snippet.title: Catherine Bach Addresses Her Weight Transformation at 69 Years Old
snippet.description:
Did you know that Catherine Bach, the iconic star who brought the character Daisy Duke to life on "The Dukes of Hazzard", has undergone a significant transformation in recent years? And did you know that her journey involved overcoming personal tragedy, adapting to physical changes, and embarking on successful entrepreneurial ventures?

▬Contents of this video▬
00:00 - Intro
00:24 - The Death Of Her Husband
04:50 - Bach’s New Look
06:04 - She’s Still Going Strong
07:51 - Outro

Like this content? Subscribe here: https://www.youtube.com/factsverse?sub_confirmation=1 

Or, watch more videos here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLkXAntdjbcSJlJnpP4FgdU0swKbnkNgJj

Become a Facts Verse member and get access to all videos that contain mature content. Use the link below to get access to even more videos, ad-free.
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCXZpQgX1897wYDLtvzmgyIA/join

In this insightful video, we explore the captivating story of Catherine Bach, who first captured the public's attention with her athletic figure and toned legs in the 1980s. Known for her iconic role as Daisy Duke, Bach experienced a significant shift in her life and physical appearance following the tragic death of her husband, Peter Lopez.

Bach faced the challenge head-on, acknowledging the weight gain that occurred in the aftermath of her husband's passing. However, she didn't let it define her. She continued to lead an active lifestyle, incorporating activities like yoga and hiking, which she often shares with her two daughters.

This video sheds light not only on Bach's physical transformation but also on her journey of resilience and reinvention in the face of adversity. We dive into the nuances of her life following the weight gain, her unyielding commitment to her daughters, and her decision to step away from romantic relationships.

But Catherine Bach's story doesn't end with personal challenges. She channeled her energy into new endeavors, evolving from an iconic television star into a successful entrepreneur. She launched her line of diamond jewelry, proving her abilities beyond the world of acting and securing her financial independence.

We'll also highlight her career's later years, touching upon her roles in "The Young and The Restless" and "Hawaii Five-0" and the 2016 television movie "My Best Friend". Despite facing industry ageism, Bach managed to leave a mark on each project she undertook, her business ventures supplementing her acting pursuits.

Join Facts Verse as we delve into the remarkable life of Catherine Bach, exploring her transformation, her responses to life's trials, and her ventures beyond acting. This isn't just a tale of a star who fell from grace only to rise again; it's a testament to the resilience of a woman who refused to be defined by her circumstances.

Catherine Bach Addresses Her Weight Transformation at 69 Years Old

snippet.title: The girls around Robert G. Miller - The Fifth Estate
snippet.description:
WARNING: This episode contains graphic sexual details.

The Fifth Estate and Radio-Canada’s investigative program Enquête examine troubling claims that a Canadian billionaire had a long history of paying teenagers for sex, going to extraordinary lengths to obscure his trail.

Read more: https://www.cbc.ca/newsinteractives/features/the-girls-around-robert-g-miller

Support is available for anyone who has experienced sexual violence. You can access crisis lines and local support services through this Government of Canada website (https://bit.ly/3D1rUmb) or the Ending Violence Association of Canada database (https://bit.ly/3ilpp67). ​​If you’re in immediate danger or fear for your safety or that of others around you, please call 911.

Update: Nov. 10, 2023

There have been some important developments since this story aired last February.

Robert G. Miller stepped down as CEO of Future Electronics and sold the company for $5 billion.

Montreal police have reopened their investigation, but no charges have been laid as of Nov. 10, 2023.

Forty-one women who say Miller paid them for sex when they were underage have launched a class-action lawsuit against the billionaire and Future Electronics. That lawsuit hasn't been certified by a judge.  

Three other individual lawsuits, with claims totalling nearly $30 million, have also been filed. Miller contests all of these lawsuits.

We’ll continue to follow the story.

snippet.title: Gilda Radner Died at 42 Years Old, Now Her Secrets Come to Light
snippet.description:
Did you know that Gilda Radner was one of the original cast members of Saturday Night Live? Or that her untimely death led to significant strides in ovarian cancer awareness and research? In this video, we delve into the life and legacy of Gilda Radner, one of the most iconic comedians to ever grace our TV screens.

▬Contents of this video▬
00:00 - Intro
00:39 - Gilda’s Unforgettable Journey: Comedy, Courage, and Change
03:48 - Personal Struggles and Final Years
05:32 - Gilda's Lasting Legacy
07:24 - Awards and Honors
07:47 - Outro

Like this content? Subscribe here: https://www.youtube.com/factsverse?sub_confirmation=1 

Or, watch more videos here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLkXAntdjbcSJlJnpP4FgdU0swKbnkNgJj

Become a Facts Verse member and get access to all videos that contain mature content. Use the link below to get access to even more videos, ad-free.
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCXZpQgX1897wYDLtvzmgyIA/join

Born in Detroit, Michigan, Gilda Radner made her way to New York City where she became a household name through her unforgettable characters on Saturday Night Live. Her comedic genius was recognized early on, landing her an Emmy Award in 1977. But beyond the laughs, Gilda's life took a tragic turn when she was diagnosed with ovarian cancer. Despite her battle with the disease, she left a lasting impact that stretches far beyond her comedy sketches.

After her death, her husband Gene Wilder took up the mantle to raise awareness about ovarian cancer. The Gilda Radner Ovarian Detection Center at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center and the Gilda Radner Hereditary Cancer Program were established to focus on early detection and screening of high-risk individuals. Gene Wilder's advocacy even reached Congress, where he testified on the importance of digging deeper into family medical histories for early diagnosis.

Gilda's influence wasn't limited to the medical world. Her story served as the inspiration behind Gilda's Club, a network of clubhouses designed to offer support to cancer patients and their families. This initiative later merged with another organization to become the Cancer Support Community, spreading its reach across the U.S. and Canada. Several streets in different cities have also been renamed "Gilda Radner Way" as a tribute to her impact.

In the realm of entertainment, tributes to Gilda haven't ceased. A three-hour block of ABC programming was dedicated to her in 2002, and she was also featured in a film tribute to female Jewish comedians in 2007. In 2003, Gilda received a posthumous star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, acknowledging her immeasurable contributions to arts and entertainment.

Posthumously, Gilda received a Grammy Award in 1990 and was inducted into the Michigan Women's Hall of Fame in 1992. Her accolades continue to roll in, solidifying her place as an icon.

Through the ups and downs, Gilda Radner's legacy lives on. Her story is a mix of laughter and tears, success and struggle, but above all, it's a story of a woman who left an indelible mark on the worlds of comedy, activism, and public health. So sit back and enjoy this deep dive into the life of Gilda Radner, a woman whose impact remains relevant to this day.

snippet.title: What to do in Palermo Sicily #palermo #italy
snippet.description:
Palermo is the capital city of the Italian island of Sicily and is the fifth-largest city in Italy. Palermo has a rich and diverse history with a warm Mediterranean atmosphere, influenced by various cultures and civilizations throughout the centuries.

Some key points about Palermo:

1. Historical Significance: Palermo has a long history dating back to the Phoenician period, and it has been ruled by various powers including the Romans, Arabs, Normans, and Spanish. Each of these influences has left its mark on the city's architecture, culture, and cuisine.

2. Architecture: The city is known for its stunning architecture, combining elements of different styles. Arab-Norman architecture is particularly prominent in Palermo, as seen in structures like the Palermo Cathedral, the Norman Palace, and the Church of San Giovanni degli Eremiti.

3. Food and Cuisine: Palermo is famous for its delicious cuisine, which is influenced by both Italian and Arabic flavors. The city is known for its street food, including arancini (rice balls), panelle (chickpea fritters), and sfincione (Sicilian pizza). Palermo's markets, such as the Vucciria and Ballarò, offer a vibrant culinary experience.

4. Monuments and Landmarks: In addition to its historical sites, Palermo is home to several impressive landmarks. The Capuchin Catacombs of Palermo, for example, house thousands of mummified bodies and are a unique attraction for visitors. The Teatro Massimo, one of the largest opera houses in Europe, is another notable landmark in the city.

5. Cultural Heritage: Palermo has a rich cultural heritage with a blend of traditions from different civilizations. The city hosts various festivals throughout the year, such as the Feast of Santa Rosalia, dedicated to the city's patron saint. The Palermo Cathedral also holds an annual procession called the Feast of Saint Rosalia.

6. Beaches: Palermo is located on the coast and offers beautiful beaches for locals and tourists to enjoy. Mondello Beach, located just outside the city, is a popular destination known for its crystal-clear waters and fine sand.

7. Museums and Art: The city has several museums and art galleries that showcase Sicilian art and history. The Regional Archaeological Museum, Palazzo Abatellis, and the Palermo Gallery of Modern Art are among the notable cultural institutions in Palermo.

Palermo is a vibrant and captivating city that offers a mix of historical, cultural, and culinary experiences. It attracts visitors with its rich heritage, stunning architecture, and warm Mediterranean atmosphere.

snippet.title: He Utterly Hated Steve McQueen, Now We Know the Reason Why
snippet.description:
Steve McQueen, often celebrated as the "King of Cool," stands as one of Hollywood's most iconic figures, leaving an indelible mark on the cinematic world with his magnetic screen presence and unparalleled charisma. Born in 1930, McQueen's journey to stardom was far from straightforward. From a tumultuous childhood to his early struggles in the entertainment industry, his rise was a testament to his tenacity and raw talent. Once he found his footing, McQueen delivered a series of unforgettable performances in films like "Bullitt," "The Great Escape," and "The Magnificent Seven." His roles often portrayed rebellious anti-heroes, mirroring his off-screen persona. However, this rebellious nature wasn't without its complications. 

▬Contents of this video▬
00:00 - Intro
00:30 - Paul Newman and McQueen
03:14 - James Garner and McQueen
04:45 - Yul Brynner and McQueen

Become a Facts Verse member and get access to all videos that contain mature content. Use the link below to get access to even more videos, a

Behind the scenes, McQueen was known for his fiery temperament, leading to bitter rivalries with some of his co-stars. These off-screen dynamics added a layer of intrigue to his public image, making him a figure of fascination both on and off the screen. Despite these controversies, there's no denying McQueen's immense contributions to the film industry. His ability to command the screen, coupled with his dedication to his craft, earned him a reputation as one of the finest actors of his generation. Tragically, McQueen's life was cut short in 1980, but his legacy endures. Today, he is remembered not just for his iconic roles but also for his influence on subsequent generations of actors and filmmakers. 

His unique blend of charisma, talent, and complexity set him apart, ensuring his place in the annals of Hollywood history as a true legend. In this video, we’ll take a look at a few of McQueen’s costars who had less than flattering things to say about the legendary star. And we’ll discuss the controversy surrounding this image and his estate after he died. Join Facts Verse, as we present: He Utterly Hated Steve McQueen, Now We Know the Reason Why!

snippet.title: Joe Mondello was FOOLED by this supposed TRUTH - Hidden Horsepower Podcast E74
snippet.description:
A, B, A testing - That’s the only proper way to test parts, right? Listen in as Billy Godlbold explains how A, B, A testing caused the legendary Joe Mondello to use the wrong pushrods for over 20 years!

Flanked by Ben Strader from EFI University, Billy and Ben explain the pitfalls of the supposed “golden rule” of dyno testing, and both share examples and explanations of why there is a better way. Ben and Billy even go through a detailed explanation of in-cylinder combustion analysis - the comparison of Top Fuel combustion to Pro Stock combustion is mind-blowing!

For more about Billy Godbold, visit https://www.godboldengineering.com

For more about Ben Strader, visit http://www.efi101.com

#camshaft #engine #enginebuilding

snippet.title: Is Drifting the next Formula? #driftmasters  #drifting
snippet.description:
Hold on tight and get ready for an adrenaline-fueled ride into the captivating world of drifting! 🌪️💨 In this awe-inspiring documentary, we uncover the untold story of how European drifting developed from the underground scene into a powerhouse motorsport that could rival even the mighty Formula 1. 🌍🏎️ Brace yourself, Formula 1, because the European Championship Drift Masters series is taking the automotive world by storm, raising the bar year after year. ⚡🚀

Join us as we take you behind the scenes of the European Drift Masters series, where we delve into the thrilling history, showcase the jaw-dropping driving skills, and reveal the intense rivalries that make this championship an absolute must-watch. Get exclusive insights from legendary drivers who have made their mark in the world of drifting, including The Shanahans, James Deane, Piotr Wiecek, and the enigmatic Naoki Nakamura. 🌟🚗💨

But it doesn't stop there! The 2023 season promises to be a mind-blowing spectacle, featuring an incredible lineup of drivers vying for the prestigious European Drift Master title. Buckle up as rising stars like Kalle Rovanpera, Ali Makhseed, Tymur Lypskyi, and Clint van Oort ignite their engines, ready to unleash their raw talent and battle it out for glory on the asphalt. 🏆🔥

Get ready for tire-smoking drift battles, heart-stopping maneuvers, and the pure exhilaration of a motorsport that pushes the limits of what's possible. From the captivating history to the behind-the-scenes stories and thrilling predictions from the drivers themselves, this adrenaline-charged documentary has it all. 🎥🔥👀

So, sit back, rev your engines, and embark on an extraordinary journey through the captivating world of drifting. Will drifting rise to become the next Formula? Find out as we witness the intensity, passion, and sheer brilliance of the European Drift Masters series. Don't forget to like, comment, and subscribe to join the conversation and be part of this mesmerizing automotive revolution! 🚘🔥💪🤝😎

Spearheaded by Bandana Street, a luminary filmmaker and drift connoisseur, we're on a mission to unify the global drift community. OMT! brings you unparalleled access to events, educational content, exclusive interviews, and the pulse of drift culture. From the streets to the tracks, and from Japan's drifting heartland to the world beyond, we are the gateway to drifting’s soul.

snippet.title: The Full Story of Haas F1 Team - Part 4 - 2019
snippet.description:
Formula 1 has often struggled to break into the American market. Historically, the United States has produced several successful drivers, but in more recent years investment and interest in Formula 1 has been superseded by domestic series like IndyCar and NASCAR. In 2014, businessman Gene Haas decided to spread his influence out from his eponymous tool manufacturing company and NASCAR team and create a Formula 1 team of his own. This is the story of Haas F1 Team.

Mike has performed in the New York Comedy Festival, Laughing Skull Comedy Festival, Asheville Comedy Festival, Cape Fear Comedy Festival, Rogue Island Comedy Festival and Skankfest. In 2021, Mike was a finalist in the 2021 New York Comedy Festival's "NY's Funniest Stand-up Competition".

He also appeared as a guest on Vice TV’s “Tumbleweeds with Killer Mike.” Brendan performed as a “New Face” in the 2022 Just For Laughs Comedy Festival. Brendan has also appeared at the Rogue Island Comedy Festival, Skankfest, and the New York Comedy Festival. Brendan has been a guest on many popular podcasts such as You Know What Dude with Robert Kelly, Are you Garbage?, Ari Shaffir’s Skeptic Tank, Tuesdays with Stories, Chrissy Chaos, The Chip Chipperson Podcast, Joe List’s Mindful Metal jacket, Guys We F****d, Calta and Kelly, and Legion of Skanks.

Mike has performed in the New York Comedy Festival, Laughing Skull Comedy Festival, Asheville Comedy Festival, Cape Fear Comedy Festival, Rogue Island Comedy Festival and Skankfest. In 2021, Mike was a finalist in the 2021 New York Comedy Festival's "NY's Funniest Stand-up Competition". 

New York City based comedian Brendan Sagalow has deftly and defiantly owned the stage for over 10 years. Brendan has written for MTV's Joking Off, appeared on the popular Barstool Sports series, “Answer the Internet,” and is a regular guest on radio shows such as the Jim and Sam Show, Barstool Sports’ KFC Radio, and The Bonfire with Big Jay Oakerson and Dan Soder. He also appeared as a guest on Vice TV’s “Tumbleweeds with Killer Mike.” Brendan performed as a “New Face” in the 2022 Just For Laughs Comedy Festival. Brendan has also appeared at the Rogue Island Comedy Festival, Skankfest, and the New York Comedy Festival. Brendan has been a guest on many popular podcasts such as You Know What Dude with Robert Kelly, Are you Garbage?, Ari Shaffir’s Skeptic Tank, Tuesdays with Stories, Chrissy Chaos, The Chip Chipperson Podcast, Joe List’s Mindful Metal jacket, Guys We F****d, Calta and Kelly, and Legion of Skanks.
